methods.description: Process for recruiting patients based on specific criteria, managing dropouts, and randomizing participants for the booster phase of the study.

steps.description: 15 patients with a history of grass pollen induced allergic rhinitis confirmed by conjunctival grass allergen challenge, skin prick test and specific IgE (Immulite), were recruited during the autumn of 2014, from the SCIT waiting list.
steps.searchable_summary: Recruit patients based on allergic rhinitis diagnosis confirmed by challenge, skin prick test, and specific IgE.

steps.description: Two participants were lost due to pregnancy and moving, respectively.
steps.searchable_summary: Handle patient dropouts from the study cohort.

steps.description: In January 2016, the remaining 12 patients were doubleblindly randomized by independent nurses with help of an online computer program, to receive either an intralymphatic booster injection of 1000 SQ-U ALK Alutard 5-grasses ® or placebo (given as ALK diluent ® ).
steps.searchable_summary: Randomize patients double-blindly to active booster or placebo using an online program.
outputs: Randomized patient groups (active booster group, placebo group)

steps.description: The 6 patients who received placebo-booster in January 2016, got an active booster injection (4b) in December 2016, (after breaking the study code), equalizing the treatment doses (Fig. 1).
steps.searchable_summary: Administer active booster to the placebo group after study code is broken.
outputs: All patients having received active booster treatment

methods.description: Administration of the initial standard course of subcutaneous immunotherapy injections.

steps.description: 13 participants were treated with three injections of 1000 SQ-U Alutard 5-grasses ®.
steps.searchable_summary: Administer three injections of SCIT treatment.
Maintain a 4-week interval between SCIT injections.

methods.description: Procedure for administering intralymphatic injections using ultrasound guidance and needle guide, including assessment of injection success.

steps.description: A needle guide was used in combination with ultrasound (BK Medical Flex Focus 500) to direct the injections directly into the lymph node.
steps.searchable_summary: Use ultrasound and needle guide for intralymphatic injection into a lymph node.

steps.description: All injections were performed by the corresponding author, assisted by the same two nurses, all with practical training from the ENT research unit at Karolinska University Hospital, Stockholm.
steps.searchable_summary: Ensure injections are performed by trained personnel.

steps.description: All injections were recorded and saved on video.
steps.searchable_summary: Record intralymphatic injection procedures on video.

steps.description: Re-examination of the films, showed that 75-80% of the injections were deposited completely inside the node, resulting in a transient nodal swelling, whereas 20-25% displayed signs of leakage into the surrounding tissues of the lymph node.
steps.searchable_summary: Analyze video recordings to assess injection deposition location (inside node vs. leakage).
outputs: Percentage of successful (intranodal) vs. unsuccessful (leakage) injections

steps.description: With a few exceptions, the same lymph node was localized in each patient at every injection.
steps.searchable_summary: Attempt to use the same lymph node for repeated injections in each patient.

methods.description: Performance of titrated grass pollen skin prick tests at multiple time points throughout the study.

steps.description: A titrated grass pollen skin prick test was performed at four occasions: At baseline, after the standard 3 first injections, before the booster injection and finally in the autumn 2016.
steps.searchable_summary: Perform titrated grass pollen skin prick tests at specified time points.

methods.method_name: Blood Sample Collection and IgG4 Measurement
methods.description: Collection, storage, and laboratory analysis of blood samples to measure allergen-specific IgG4 levels.

steps.description: Blood samples for allergen specific IgG4 (Immulite-technique, Siemens, expressed in μg/L) to timothy and birch measurements, were obtained at 5 occasions...
steps.searchable_summary: Collect blood samples for allergen-specific IgG4 measurement.

steps.description: and stored frozen at minus 20 degrees: In January 2015 (baseline), in April 2015 (1 month after the 3d injection), in December 2015 (before randomized booster-injection, in January 2016), in March 2016 (approx. 2 months after booster injection), and in October-November 2016.
steps.searchable_summary: Store blood samples frozen at -20°C and collect at specified time points.

steps.description: In these 12 participants, all but one IgG4-sample has been included in the final analysis. One IgG4-sample was omitted due to a several months relapsing leg-erysipelas during the summer and autumn of 2016, preceding the last blood sampling.
steps.searchable_summary: Exclude specific blood samples from analysis based on predefined criteria (e.g., medical condition).
outputs: Filtered set of blood samples for analysis

steps.description: ...for allergen specific IgG4 (Immulite-technique, Siemens, expressed in μg/L) to timothy and birch measurements...
steps.searchable_summary: Measure allergen-specific IgG4 levels in blood samples using Immulite technique.

methods.description: Collection of daily patient-reported data on rhinoconjunctivitis symptoms and medication use during grass pollen seasons via an online questionnaire.

steps.description: 10 of the 12 participants (6 with active booster and 4 with placebo booster), answered a daily online Google form questionnaire during the grass pollen seasons of 2015 and 2016 (37-41 days with about 75% response rate the same day, during the months of June and July, when the birch pollen season was terminated).
steps.searchable_summary: Administer a daily online questionnaire to collect symptom and medication data during pollen season.

steps.description: The questionnaire scored the rhinoconjunctivitis symptoms (4 nasal and 3 ocular symptoms each ranging from 0 = no symptoms to 3 = severe symptoms), and use of medication, without dose specification.
steps.searchable_summary: Collect symptom scores (0-3 scale) for nasal and ocular symptoms and record medication use.

methods.description: Repeated administration of a retrospective survey to collect subjective patient feedback on overall treatment effectiveness and experience over multiple years.

steps.description: Finally a repeated retrospective survey was sent out eleven times during the summers of 2015-2018, with 5 identical questions, asking the participants to estimate in a subjective manner, how they had responded to the treatment in general (if the injections had been effective, estimation of less or more symptoms, less or more use of medication and tiredness, compared to 2014 and before), and if they wanted to recommend ILIT to a friend.
steps.searchable_summary: Administer a repeated retrospective survey to collect subjective treatment response feedback.
outputs: Survey responses (subjective treatment effectiveness, symptom/medication/tiredness change, recommendation)
